name: Chino Commercial Bank, N.A.
description_short: Chino Commercial Bank, N.A. is an FDIC-insured bank offering personal and business checking, savings, loans, and merchant services with multiple convenient locations.
description_long:
Chino Commercial Bank, N.A. is a full-service national bank (indicated by the N.A. designation) that serves both individual consumers and business clients. The bank is FDIC-insured, providing deposit protection up to regulatory limits. Based on their website, they maintain multiple physical branch locations and have been operating long enough to establish an investor relations program and community presence.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of personal banking products including checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit, individual retirement accounts, personal loans, and online/mobile banking platforms. For business clients, they provide business checking and savings accounts, commercial loans, business lines of credit, SBA 504 loans, equipment financing, commercial real estate financing, online cash management tools, remote deposit capture, and merchant services with point-of-sale hardware support. They also offer specialized banking solutions for medical professionals.

Chino Commercial Bank distinguishes itself through several business-focused offerings: their emphasis on commercial real estate lending, SBA loan products, and merchant services suggests a bank positioned for small to mid-sized business customers. They offer modern conveniences like Zelle money transfers, CardValet fraud protection, and mobile banking alongside traditional services. The bank also provides resources for loan modification and foreclosure prevention, indicating community-focused lending practices.

As a regional bank with physical locations, Chino Commercial Bank operates in a competitive landscape dominated by large national banks and online banking options. Their strength appears to be in personalized business lending and maintaining local branch relationships. However, the website provides limited information about specific interest rates, fees, loan terms, or geographic service areas, which would be important factors for prospective customers comparing options.
services: Business loans and lines of credit
Commercial real estate financing and refinancing
Online banking and mobile banking platforms
pros: FDIC-insured deposits provide federal protection for consumer accounts
Offers specialized banking solutions for medical professionals, a niche market segment
Provides SBA 504 loans and equipment financing for small business expansion
Multiple convenient branch locations for in-person banking services
Modern digital tools including Zelle transfers, CardValet fraud alerts, and mobile banking
Merchant services with hardware support for business sales and payment processing
Online cash management platform allows businesses to manage multiple accounts in one place
Loan modification and foreclosure prevention resources available to homeowners
cons: Website lacks specific information about interest rates, APYs, and fee schedules, making rate comparison difficult
No pricing or terms disclosed for business loans, commercial real estate financing, or merchant services
Geographic service areas not clearly defined on website, limiting clarity on where customers can access physical branches
Limited details about minimum deposit requirements, account minimums, or eligibility criteria for various products
No information about competitive advantages in rates or terms compared to other regional or national banks
best_for: Small to mid-sized business owners seeking personalized commercial lending relationships
Medical professionals looking for industry-specialized banking solutions
Customers who prefer local branch banking with community relationships
Businesses needing merchant services, equipment financing, or SBA loan programs
diagnosis: Chino Commercial Bank is best for business owners and professionals who value personalized local banking relationships and need commercial lending products like SBA loans or equipment financing. The main caveat is that the website provides virtually no pricing information, interest rates, or fee details, requiring prospective customers to contact the bank directly to compare terms with competitors.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
